text.text: Good variety of items, some of the cutest stationary and items for the kitchen. They had claw machines in the back and a variety of Japanese ice creams. I’m giving it a 3 star because the staff made us feel very uncomfortable walking around and behind us all the time we were at the store 😔 I get it we are a family with kids and idk if being Hispanics made any difference to the but it was very uncomfortable to have the staff be “arranging products” next to us or walking by over and over near us. We paid for every single item we purchased but overall experience was a 3 star. Some prices were decent but others were too expensive like all the little bags I wanted for pencils or make up, they were all $20 or $25 dollars. Some items felt over priced. We mostly came for those trendy ice creams that were like $6 or more each. We found lychee, strawberry and pineapple flavor. They also had mango and peach but we had already purchased those in the past for much less at another store. There was plenty of free parking in a structure where you can enter straight to the store. They had some cute characters but couldn’t take pictures with them 😢 wish that would’ve been an option but that’s ok I guess. They had this sign at the register “Free gift with purchase of $30” - 🙄 a bag of old Christmas themed caramel popcorn 🍿 but I guess it’s free just that sign makes it seem like it’s gonna be an actual gift, they might as well put on there free bag of popcorn instead of making sign so deceiving.

text.text: Love this place. We were in town and we missed Japan so much that I knew I needed to come here. Its an amazing store. A definite place to come check out.

originalText.text: Love this place. We were in town and we missed Japan so much that I knew I needed to come here. Its an amazing store. A definite place to come check out.

text.text:
First time at Ebisu Life Store and BABYYYY… instant obsession 😍✨
Took my baby girl and it was giving cute, cozy, main-character energy from the second we walked in.
We definitely helped ourselves to the peach + mango ice cream (don’t judge us 🍑🥭), bought a couple spicy curry noodle cups that had us like “okayyyy flavor 😮‍🔥,” and spent way too much time swooning over all the Sanrio characters because nostalgia stays undefeated 💕.
AND let’s talk about the cutest arcade ever 🎮—my baby was living her best life window shopping the games and honestly… so was I. Fun, playful, comforting, and lowkey dangerous if you love cute things and snacks.
If you’re in Alhambra and not pulling up to Ebisu Life Store, you’re playing yourself. She’s fun, she’s cozy, she’s THAT GIRL. First visit but absolutely not the last. Period🤏🏽🤏🏽🤏🏽

text.text: Great experience! Found a great selection of japanese treats i thought i would never get to try unless i went to japan. They have all kinds of things hello kitty! Candy, drinks, household items they got it. Highly recommend you visit them. The staff is very friendly and helpful!

text.text: Their specialty is products from Japan, and they do have a lot to choose from. It’s located on the ground floor of a large multifamily building and there a lot of covered free parking attached. Inside is not as large as I was expecting, but it does have a lot of great product options. There are multiple aisles of toys, lots of health and beauty products, stationary, snacks, and frozen foods. I was especially glad to see the viral strawberry ice cream in the shape of a strawberry, they are delicious. I have had the mango and peach before many times, but for some reason, I’ve never seen the strawberry before. They also have a lot of great snack options and the store was clean and quiet during our visit so absolutely no complaints. I wish it would be a little bit larger, but they still have a lot of great options and I would recommend visiting.
